title: Agent test exchange: server-side rate-limit store in AppState
description: Add challenge_rate_limits: tokio::sync::RwLock<HashMap<String, chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>>> to AppState in acc-server/src/state.rs. Key format: '{agent_a}:{agent_b}' where the two names are sorted alphabetically so the pair is commutative. Add a new validation step to bus_send() in routes/bus.rs: if msg type is 'agent.test_challenge', extract from+to fields, look up the pair key in challenge_rate_limits, if last challenge was < 3600s ago return 429 with {error: 'rate_limited', retry_after_seconds: N}. On pass, update the map with the current timestamp.

title: Agent test exchange: failure handling — go offline, file defect tasks, self-repair
description: In the test_submit handler, after execution: (1) calculate failure rate = failed_tests / total_tests, (2) if failure_rate > 0.3 (configurable via ACC_TEST_FAILURE_THRESHOLD env var, default 0.3), the agent must: (a) write a quench file to take itself offline for 1 hour, (b) POST one fleet task per failed test (task_type=work, priority=1, title='Fix: {test title}', description includes command+expected+actual), (c) log 'TEST EXCHANGE FAILED: going offline to fix {n} defects', (3) if failure_rate <= threshold: log pass summary. This entire flow is fire-and-forget inside a tokio::spawn.

title: Agent test exchange: test execution on the challenged agent
description: Handle 'agent.test_challenge' bus messages in acc-agent/src/bus.rs. On receipt: (1) verify message is addressed to this agent, (2) iterate through tests[] array, (3) execute each test's command via tokio::process::Command with a 30s timeout per test, (4) record pass/fail, actual exit code, stdout, stderr for each, (5) POST an 'agent.test_submit' bus message back to the initiator with full results. A test passes if exit_code matches expected_exit_code and output contains expected_output_contains (if set). Run tests sequentially to avoid interference.

title: Agent test exchange: challenge initiation handler in acc-agent
description: Add a challenge initiator to acc-agent/src/bus.rs. Handle the new bus message type 'agent.test_challenge.initiate' (to=self). On receipt: (1) pick a target peer that is online and not self, (2) read the shared AgentFS workspace to understand what code exists, (3) construct a structured JSON test plan with specific test cases (concrete commands, expected outputs, edge cases to probe), (4) POST an agent.test_challenge bus message addressed to the target. The test plan JSON format: {tests: [{id, title, command, expected_exit_code, expected_output_contains}]}. Rate-limit 429 from the server is handled by logging and dropping — no retry.

title: Agent test exchange: rate-limit enforcement (max 1/hour per agent pair)
description: Implement rate-limit storage and enforcement for test challenges. The server should track the last challenge timestamp for each (initiator, target) pair in memory (a HashMap keyed by sorted pair). POST /api/bus/send with type=agent.test_challenge should be rejected with 429 Too Many Requests if the pair has challenged within the last 3600 seconds. Return the seconds-until-next-allowed in the error body. The rate-limit store lives in AppState and does not persist across restarts.

title: Agent test exchange: bus message schema (test_challenge, test_submit, test_results)
description: Define the three AgentBus message types that drive the peer test exchange protocol. test_challenge: initiator→target, contains test specs devised by initiator (list of test cases with descriptions, expected behaviors, or runnable commands). test_submit: target→initiator, contains test execution results (pass/fail per case, stdout/stderr, exit codes). test_results: final summary posted to bus so all agents can observe. Use the existing MediaType enum for any binary payloads. Add a new rate-limit entry to app state so the server can enforce the once-per-hour per-pair constraint at the /api/bus/send level.

title: [epic] Peer agent test exchange protocol
description: Any two agents in the fleet can declare a priority test exchange with each other, rate-limited to at most once per hour per pair. The initiating agent devises tests using its knowledge of the codebase, sends them to the target, and the target executes them. If the test run reveals significant failures, the failing agent must take itself offline, raise beads tasks for all defects, and start fixing the highest-priority items automatically.

title: AgentBus: enc field enforcement and base64 binary decode in dispatch()
description:
## Why
The enc field exists in the AgentBus spec but is currently ignored by agents. Binary payloads sent as base64 are passed through as raw base64 strings instead of decoded bytes.

## What
In agent/acc-agent/src/bus.rs dispatch(): when enc=='base64', base64-decode payload before routing. On outbound binary messages, base64-encode and set enc='base64'. Add a round-trip test: send message with enc=base64 payload, verify decoded bytes match original.
close_reason: Implemented in feat(agentbus) commit 8f409d2: bus_types.rs MediaType enum, blob upload/download/TTL/access-control routes, DLQ endpoints, enc enforcement in bus_send and agent dispatch

title: AgentBus: access control on blob downloads
description:
## Why
Blobs may contain private sensor data or agent-specific payloads that should not be visible to all agents.

## What
Upload accepts optional allowed_agents: ["boris", "natasha"] list. GET /api/bus/blobs/:id/download checks requesting agent identity (from Bearer token) against allowed_agents. Empty list = public. Return 403 for unauthorized. Add tests for authorized and unauthorized cases.
close_reason: Implemented in feat(agentbus) commit 8f409d2: bus_types.rs MediaType enum, blob upload/download/TTL/access-control routes, DLQ endpoints, enc enforcement in bus_send and agent dispatch

title: AgentBus: blob TTL, expiry background task, and DELETE endpoint
description:
## Why
Blobs accumulate indefinitely without TTL, eventually filling disk.

## What
POST /api/bus/blobs accepts optional ttl_seconds (default 86400). Hourly background task deletes expired blobs and fires bus:blob_expired events. Add GET /api/bus/blobs to list stored blobs with expiry. Add DELETE /api/bus/blobs/:id for manual deletion. Write tests covering expiry timing logic.
close_reason: Implemented in feat(agentbus) commit 8f409d2: bus_types.rs MediaType enum, blob upload/download/TTL/access-control routes, DLQ endpoints, enc enforcement in bus_send and agent dispatch

title: AgentBus: chunked binary upload endpoint for large blobs
description:
## Why
Agents need to exchange audio clips, video frames, and images that exceed HTTP body limits for inline base64.

## What
Implement POST /api/bus/blobs supporting multipart chunked upload. Fields: blob_id, chunk_index, total_chunks, mime_type, data (binary). Server reassembles chunks in ~/.acc/blobs/{blob_id}/. On final chunk, write metadata.json and fire bus:blob_ready event with {blob_id, mime_type, size_bytes, agent}. Max blob size configurable (default 2GB). Add integration tests.
close_reason: Implemented in feat(agentbus) commit 8f409d2: bus_types.rs MediaType enum, blob upload/download/TTL/access-control routes, DLQ endpoints, enc enforcement in bus_send and agent dispatch

title: AgentBus: shared MediaType enum and JSON schema registry
description:
## Why
Every agent currently matches bus message types as raw strings, leading to silent discards on typos or unknown types. A shared enum eliminates that class of bug.

## What
Define a Rust enum MediaType in acc-server/src/bus_types.rs covering all supported types:
- text/plain, text/markdown, text/html, application/json
- audio/wav, audio/mp3, audio/ogg, audio/flac
- video/mp4, video/webm, video/ogg
- image/png (primary), image/jpeg, image/gif, image/webp, image/svg+xml
- application/octet-stream

Auto-generate JSON schema from the enum and write it to agent/acc-agent/media-types.json. Agent dispatch() parses type tag against the enum; unknown types are rejected to DLQ, not silently dropped. Add unit tests for round-trip serialization.
close_reason: Implemented in feat(agentbus) commit 8f409d2: bus_types.rs MediaType enum, blob upload/download/TTL/access-control routes, DLQ endpoints, enc enforcement in bus_send and agent dispatch

title: AgentBus: full multimedia data bus (text, audio, video, 2D graphics)
description:
## Why this exists

AgentBus currently handles text messages only. The original design goal was a fully typed, multimedia-capable inter-agent data bus supporting text, audio, video, and 2D graphics. Agents need to exchange sensor data, rendered frames, audio clips, and structured documents without encoding workarounds or out-of-band file transfers.

## What needs to be done

Design and implement AgentBus v2 as a fully multimedia-capable data bus with a canonical, versioned media-type registry shared by all agents and the server. Every message carries an explicit, enumerated type tag so agents can dispatch, validate, and route without guessing at content.

### Scope

1. **Shared media type registry** — a single source of truth (Rust enum + generated JSON schema) defining all supported types:
   - Text: `text/plain`, `text/markdown`, `text/html`, `application/json`
   - Audio: `audio/wav`, `audio/mp3`, `audio/ogg`, `audio/flac`
   - Video: `video/mp4`, `video/webm`, `video/ogg`
   - 2D Graphics: `image/png` (primary), `image/jpeg`, `image/gif`, `image/webp`, `image/svg+xml`
   - Binary/blob: `application/octet-stream` (typed fallback)
   - All types versioned; unknown types rejected at the bus boundary

2. **Chunked binary upload endpoint** — POST /api/bus/blobs with multipart chunks; server reassembles and fires `bus:blob_ready` on completion

3. **Blob lifecycle** — TTL (configurable, default 24h), expiry background task, DELETE endpoint, `bus:blob_expired` event

4. **Access control** — upload specifies `allowed_agents` list; download enforces it via Bearer token agent identity; 403 on violation

5. **`enc` field enforcement** — messages with binary payloads use `enc: base64`; agent `dispatch()` decodes before routing; encoder sets field on send

6. **Agent-side typed dispatch** — `dispatch()` routes on the enum variant, not raw string matching; unknown types go to dead-letter queue

7. **Dead-letter queue** — failed dispatches written to `~/.acc/bus-dlq.jsonl`; POST /api/bus/dlq/redeliver; GET /api/bus/dlq list

8. **SPEC.md v2** — complete protocol specification covering envelope schema, all type definitions, blob endpoints, DLQ, access control, sequence diagrams

## Acceptance criteria

- All media types defined in a shared Rust enum; JSON schema auto-generated and checked into repo
- Agents reject messages with unrecognized type tags at parse time (not silently discard)
- Binary payloads round-trip correctly: encode → upload → download → decode produces identical bytes
- Blob TTL expiry fires `bus:blob_expired` and removes storage within 5 minutes of TTL
- Access control returns 403 for unauthorized agent downloads
- DLQ captures all dispatch failures; redelivery replays them successfully
- SPEC.md v2 documents every endpoint and message type
acceptance_criteria:
- Shared MediaType enum exists in acc-server and is published as JSON schema
- All 6 existing AgentBus tasks (chunked upload, TTL, access control, enc field, DLQ, SPEC.md) are children of this epic and complete
- Integration test: png image sent agent→server→agent, decoded bytes match original
- Integration test: audio/wav round-trip identical
- No agent silently discards an unknown type (logged to DLQ instead)
close_reason: All implementation tasks complete

title: fix: broken CI workflows (nonexistent packages, missing agent cargo test)
description: ci.yml references nonexistent tests/ and services/accfs; release.yml targets squirrelchat-server and dashboard-server packages that don't exist; ci.yml omits agent from cargo test; wasm-build.yml tests dashboard-server package that doesn't exist.
close_reason: Closed

title: test: synthetic black-box probe suite targeting live cluster
description: No end-to-end tests exist. Create tests/ directory with pytest probe suite: bus ping, agentFS read/write, queue echo task, work splitting across agents, hermes routing, exec registry, failure recovery. Configurable via ACC_URL + ACC_AGENT_TOKEN env.
close_reason: Closed

title: test: server integration test infrastructure (axum-test + fixtures)
description: acc-server/Cargo.toml has no dev-dependencies. Zero endpoints tested. Add axum-test, tempfile, httpmock and write integration tests for auth, queue cycle, exec routing, and bus. Start with TestServer builder and fixture helpers.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: bus listener SSE reconnect backoff is clobbered immediately
description: bus.rs:78 resets retry_delay to 5s right after doubling it, defeating exponential backoff. Remove the reset line.
close_reason: Closed

title: feat: hermes-worker poll service (systemd + launchd)
description: acc-agent hermes --poll exists but no service unit starts it. Add acc-hermes-worker.service and com.acc.hermes-worker.plist so hermes-capable nodes pick up hermes-tagged queue items.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: resolve capability names to agent names in exec routing
description: POST /api/exec with targets:["hermes"] silently drops because no agent is registered under that name. The server must resolve capability labels to actual agent names before fan-out, using the agents state.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: remove duplicate acc-exec-listen service (alias of bus-listener)
description: acc-exec-listen.service and com.acc.exec-listen.plist both run acc-agent listen which is an alias for acc-agent bus. Both are already covered by acc-bus-listener. Duplicate causes double execution.
close_reason: Closed

title: feat: command execution registry replacing raw shell exec
description: Replace raw /bin/sh -c code with a named-command registry (commands.toml). Dispatch via Command::new without a shell. Commands have typed param schemas, platform metadata, and arg templates. HMAC then secures a finite vocabulary, not an infinite shell surface.
close_reason: Closed

title: claude CLI: resolve anthropic-beta header incompatibility with NVIDIA LiteLLM proxy
close_reason: nvidia-proxy.py installed on all agents via migration 0017 — strips anthropic-beta before forwarding to NVIDIA LiteLLM. Deployed to rocky and ollama via SSH, bullwinkle/natasha/boris via bus exec.

title: ollama-server: install GitHub deploy key for git push from queue-worker
close_reason: Deploy key (id_ecdsa) was already installed; remote switched from HTTPS to SSH git@github.com:jordanhubbard/ACC.git — git push now works

title: End-to-end smoke test of shared client migration
description: After all ports land: verify acc-cli list/get/tail still work against a running acc-server; verify acc-agent claims a task and posts heartbeat/complete; verify hermes acc_shared_memory stores+retrieves a memory; verify SSE bus tail receives events. Document what was checked.

title: Port hermes acc_shared_memory plugin to Python acc_client
description: Replace the curl-subprocess _http helper in hermes/contrib/plugins/acc_shared_memory/__init__.py with calls through the new acc_client package. Preserve existing behavior: pre_llm_call semantic search with tiered rendering (≤1200 tokens), post_llm_call throttled store, on_session_end archival. Stop blocking pre_llm_call on synchronous curl.

title: Create Python acc_client package at clients/python/acc_client/
description: httpx-based Python client mirroring the Rust acc-client surface. v1 scope: memory (search/store), heartbeat, tasks (list/claim/complete), queue (list/claim/complete/fail), bus (send; SSE stream optional). Sync + async variants. Token resolution: ACC_TOKEN → CCC_AGENT_TOKEN → ACC_AGENT_TOKEN → ~/.acc/.env. Install into hermes venv (update deploy/setup-hermes-venv.sh).

title: Port acc-agent to use acc-client
description: Replace direct reqwest calls in agent/acc-agent/src/{tasks,queue,hermes}.rs with acc-client. Covers: GET/POST /api/tasks + claim/unclaim/complete/review-result, GET /api/queue, POST /api/item/{id}/claim|complete|fail|comment|keepalive, POST /api/heartbeat/{agent}, SSE /bus/stream for dispatch nudges. Preserve existing retry/backoff and fire-and-forget semantics.

title: Port acc-cli to use acc-client
description: Replace direct reqwest calls in acc-cli/src/main.rs with acc-client. Subcommands touched: tasks (list/get/create/complete/cancel/claim/unclaim), projects (CRUD), agents (list/get), bus (tail/send/messages), queue (list/get). Keep CLI-specific concerns (arg parsing, table/JSON formatting) in acc-cli. Verify SSE 'bus tail' behavior preserved.

title: Create acc-client Rust crate (HTTP + SSE)
description: New crate acc-client depending on acc-model. Modules: auth (token resolution: flag → env → ~/.acc/.env), error (typed errors incl. Conflict/Locked), tasks, queue, items (includes heartbeat), bus (send/messages + SSE stream), projects, agents, memory. Client struct holds base_url + token + reqwest::Client. Expose SSE as impl Stream<Item = BusMsg>. Unit tests with mock server where feasible.

title: Convert ACC Rust crates to a Cargo workspace + add acc-model crate
description: Promote acc-cli, acc-server, and agent/acc-agent into members of a single Cargo workspace at repo root. Introduce a new acc-model crate holding shared types (Task, QueueItem, Agent, Project, BusMsg, etc.) used by both server and client. Server depends on acc-model; client will depend on acc-model in the next issue. Update deploy scripts and Makefile targets that build specific crates.
close_reason: Workspace conversion landed in commit ce5e810. Root Cargo.toml with four members (acc-cli, acc-server, agent/acc-agent, acc-model); agent inner workspace removed; build scripts updated; cargo check --workspace green. acc-model is a skeleton — types migrate in CCC-e37 alongside the acc-client crate.

title: Epic: extract shared ACC client libraries (Rust + Python)
description: Eliminate HTTP-client drift between acc-cli, acc-agent, and hermes acc_shared_memory plugin by extracting shared libraries. Decisions locked: (1) acc-model crate for shared types between server and client, (2) include SSE bus streaming in v1, (3) Python package lives at clients/python/acc_client/ in this repo, (4) convert the three Rust crates into a Cargo workspace as part of the refactor.

title: Hub UI: 2D grid of VT100 terminal windows — one per agent (80x24, interactive hermes-agent CLI)
description:
Add a terminal multiplexer view to the single-pane-of-glass hub dashboard. Each registered agent gets its own 80x24 VT100-emulated terminal pane arranged in a responsive 2D grid. The user can click any pane and type commands that are routed to that agent's live hermes-agent CLI session.

**What needs to be built**

Server side (acc-server):
- New WebSocket endpoint: GET /api/agents/:name/pty — upgrades to WS, proxies a PTY session running on the agent's host. The agent must be reachable via the existing SSH config (ssh_host/ssh_port/ssh_user from agent registration).
- PTY bridge: on WS connect, SSH to the agent host and spawn 'hermes' inside a PTY with TERM=xterm-256color, COLUMNS=80, LINES=24. Forward all WS binary frames to PTY stdin; forward all PTY stdout to WS frames. On WS close, kill the PTY.
- Size tracking: accept a WS control message {type:'resize', cols:N, rows:N} and call ioctl TIOCSWINSZ on the PTY fd.
- Auth: same bearer-token check as other endpoints.

Agent side:
- No agent-side changes required: the server SSHes in and spawns hermes directly.

Dashboard UI (routes/ui.rs + static assets):
- Add a /terminals route (or a tab in the existing dashboard) that renders the 2D grid.
- Use xterm.js (CDN import, no build step needed) for each terminal pane.
- Grid layout: CSS grid, auto-fill columns, each cell 80ch wide, 24 lines tall (approx 560x408px at 7x17px font).
- On page load: open one WebSocket per online agent to /api/agents/:name/pty.
- Clicking a pane focuses it; keyboard input goes to that pane's WS.
- Pane header shows agent name + online/offline badge.
- Graceful degradation: offline agents show a greyed-out placeholder, reconnect button.

**Acceptance criteria**
- User can open /terminals in a browser and see a grid of agent terminal panes.
- Typing in a focused pane sends keystrokes to that agent's hermes CLI and output appears in real time.
- Resizing the browser window re-flows the grid; each terminal stays 80x24 internally.
- Closing the browser tab or navigating away cleanly terminates all PTY sessions.
- At least one integration test verifies the /api/agents/:name/pty endpoint upgrades to WS (can use tokio-tungstenite in tests).

title: Agent test exchange: integration tests
description: Write acc-server/tests/test_exchange_test.rs covering: (1) POST agent.test_challenge with valid from/to fields is accepted (200), (2) second challenge within 3600s returns 429 with retry_after_seconds, (3) challenge after 3600s is accepted again (mock time by directly mutating rate_limits store via a test-only helper), (4) challenge with from==to returns 422 (self-challenge disallowed). Write agent unit tests in acc-agent/src/bus.rs covering: test_execute_challenge_runs_each_test, test_pass_rate_below_threshold_triggers_offline, test_pass_rate_above_threshold_no_offline.

title: AgentBus: dead-letter queue for failed dispatch
description:
## Why
Failed bus message dispatches are currently silently lost with no way to investigate or replay.

## What
When dispatch() returns error or panics, write message to ~/.acc/bus-dlq.jsonl with timestamp, error, retry_count. Add POST /api/bus/dlq/redeliver to replay entries (with optional max_age_seconds filter). Add GET /api/bus/dlq to list pending entries. Write tests.
close_reason: Implemented in feat(agentbus) commit 8f409d2: bus_types.rs MediaType enum, blob upload/download/TTL/access-control routes, DLQ endpoints, enc enforcement in bus_send and agent dispatch

title: AgentBus: SPEC.md v2 — complete multimedia protocol specification
description:
## Why
The current spec is incomplete and does not document blob endpoints, type registry, DLQ, or access control.

## What
Rewrite agent/acc-agent/SPEC.md as AgentBus v2 specification covering: (1) versioned message envelope schema with all fields, (2) full MediaType enum listing, (3) blob upload/download/TTL/access-control endpoints, (4) enc field encoding rules, (5) DLQ behavior, (6) sequence diagrams for text message, binary blob transfer, and DLQ redeliver flows. Blocked by all other AgentBus tasks.
close_reason: Superseded: will write combined SPEC.md after peer test exchange protocol is implemented
